evals.prompt: Use $ship to publish the current scoped change as a PR.
expectations: Inspects worktree, branch, remote, GitHub auth, base branch, and validation evidence.
Stages and commits only intended work when uncommitted changes belong to the PR.
Pushes the branch with upstream tracking.
Creates or reuses a normal non-draft PR by default and reports the PR URL without merging.

evals.prompt: Use $ship draft to publish this exploratory change as a PR that should not be landed yet.
expectations: Creates or preserves a draft PR only because the draft modifier was explicit.
Does not request Codex review or mark the PR ready.
Reports the PR URL, draft state, validation evidence, and why it should not be landed yet.
Does not merge.

evals.prompt: Use $ship review to publish this change and ask Codex to review it.
expectations: Publishes or reuses the PR and marks it ready when the review request requires that state.
Posts the Codex review request as a separate PR comment using the documented trigger.
Does not put the review trigger in the PR body.
Keeps any watch loop bounded and does not merge.

evals.prompt: Use $ship to open a PR for the API fix, but the branch also has unrelated styling cleanup.
expectations: Detects mixed scope before staging or pushing.
Publishes only the intended slice when it can be separated safely.
Asks one concise question when the intended slice cannot be separated safely.
Does not create a broad mixed PR.

evals.prompt: Use $ship to publish and merge this change.
expectations: Publishes or updates the PR and verifies the URL and head/base state.
Does not run gh pr merge from the ship workflow.
Hands the merge decision to $land-pr after publication is complete.
Reports validation evidence and any blockers before the landing handoff.
